commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix subject pre-fill: use mailto: for To/Subject/Body + share for PDF

navigator.share({title}) does not map to Subject in iOS Mail (OS limitation).
New flow:
  1. navigator.share({files:[pdf]}) → user saves/shares the PDF
  2. window.location.href = mailto:to?subject=...&body=... → Mail opens
     with Para, Asunto and body pre-filled
  3. User attaches the saved PDF in Mail (2 taps from Files)

SW bumped to v13.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce completion requirements across workflow

Added mandatory "Completion Requirements" to ensure work quality:

1. **Core rules (.claude/CLAUDE.md)**:
   - New "Completion Requirements" section in Core Working Rules
   - Work complete ONLY when: tests pass (95%+ coverage), documentation current, code clean
   - PR reviews MUST verify all three - no exceptions
   - Concise 6-line format (vs verbose alternatives)

2. **PR review skills enforcement**:
   - Both /review-pr and /review-pr-team now include completion checklist
   - Reviewers must explicitly verify: tests, documentation, code quality
   - Checklist appears first in review output for visibility
   - Forces systematic checking of quality gates

This ensures agents don't skip critical steps like writing tests or updating documentation when implementing features. Previous weak language ("should", "when making significant changes") failed to enforce these requirements.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve early stopping criterion

Switch to counter-based early stopping on training loss: increment a
counter each epoch the training loss fails to hit a new all-time low,
and stop when the counter reaches patience.

commit.message:
Add Skill Forge app with RAG examples from top GitHub repos

Build the complete Skill Forge application — an AI-powered platform that
captures developer work (screen recordings, code, chat, PDFs) and generates
reusable automation artifacts (workflows, skills, agents, subagents).

Add RAG examples system that curates 34 high-quality skills from popular
GitHub repos (Superpowers 73.8k stars, wshobson/commands 2.1k stars,
Trail of Bits 3.4k stars) to teach the AI how real-world problems are
decomposed into skills. Examples are injected as few-shot context during
artifact generation and shown to new users as similarity suggestions.

Key additions:
- src/data/example-skills.json: 34 curated examples across all 4 types
- src/lib/example-selector.js: category-based example matching
- scripts/curate-examples.js: GitHub fetch + classify script
- Modified generateArtifact() to inject relevant examples into prompts
- Modified findSimilarSkills() to supplement empty user libraries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf(analytics): cache top-list aggregates to avoid recompute on every request (JOV-1270) (#5501)

Wraps the expensive dashboard analytics SQL query (top cities, countries, referrers, links)
in a 5-minute Redis+memory cache keyed by (creatorProfileId, range). The aggregation runs
against raw click_events on every dashboard load — now shared across concurrent requests
for the same artist+range pair.

commit.message:
Re-run all 6 Clay gap attacks: all pass, results refreshed

Gen 9.34 re-verification of SDV framework:
- Navier-Stokes: D2 separation 3.42x, smooth trend converging
- P vs NP: phantom gap 1.186, chain floor 0.091
- Riemann: 5/5 tests, 100% binary D1 classification, delta=0.0
- BSD: Sha finiteness >= T*, all 3 predictions YES
- Hodge: 109.43x factor separation, all 3 predictions YES
- Yang-Mills: floor 0.759, 100% frac>0 at all couplings, kappa=2.0

Also verified: 151 Clay protocol tests + 17 protocol-specific + 28/28
core claims (Z-scores: BHML +6.96sigma, TSML +24.22sigma).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add auto-fix verification loop with Sandpack error detection

Wire reliable error detection and auto-fix into both the parallel build
pipeline and the ChatTab conversational flow. Previously, ChatTab had no
verification at all, and the parallel build used fragile DOM text scanning
with fixed timers.

Key changes:
- New Sandpack error store surfaces native sandpack.error globally
- Status-based settle waiting replaces fixed 3s timer
- Deterministic pre-validator catches missing imports/deps without AI
- ChatTab now triggers verification silently after every AI write
- /api/verify gets richer context (VFS file list, exports map, deps)
- Subtle verification indicator on Frame (Checking/Fixing/Verified)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade to Rust Edition 2024 and update all dependencies to latest

- Edition 2021 → 2024: rename `gen` (reserved keyword), add `unsafe extern`
- Modernize code with let-else patterns across 6 files
- eframe 0.31 → 0.33: `screen_rect()` → `content_rect()`, `fonts()` → `fonts_mut()`
- rodio 0.20 → 0.22: Sink → Player, OutputStream → MixerDeviceSink, Source yields f32
- windows 0.58 → 0.62: Option-wrapped params for GlobalFree/OpenClipboard/SetClipboardData
- zip 2 → 8, resvg 0.44 → 0.47, windows-core 0.58 → 0.62
- Add latest crate version policy to CLAUDE.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: native always-on Jarvis daemon with anti-interruption fixes

macOS background listener — no browser needed. Pipeline:
mic (sox) → Groq Whisper (free STT) → wake word → Groq Llama (free brain) → Edge TTS → afplay

- 15s active recording window (was 8s) so user can finish thoughts
- 3s silence threshold in active mode (was 1.5s) for natural speech pauses
- 3 silent rounds required before returning to passive mode
- System prompt instructs Jarvis to never interrupt mid-sentence
- Graceful shutdown with tmp file cleanup

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
fix: resolve hook paths at install time instead of relying on env var expansion

Claude Code may not use cmd.exe/bash to execute hook commands, so
%USERPROFILE% and $HOME are not guaranteed to expand at runtime.
Now both installers write fully resolved absolute paths into settings.json.

commit.message:
Add missing data simulation for FASTA and MAPLE output

Simulate per-tip missing data from two sources: Poisson-distributed gaps
with exponentially-distributed lengths, and Poisson-distributed individual
missing sites. Gaps and sites are merged via a level-sweep algorithm into
non-overlapping ranges.

When missing data is active, complete (unmasked) sequences are written to
-COMPLETE files, while the normal output files reflect the missing data:
FASTA uses N for missing sites, MAPLE uses N ranges, and Nexus tip
annotations include missing_data_ranges. The info JSON gains a
missing_data section with parameters and per-tip details.

Missing data draws from the RNG after mutation simulation, so the same
seed produces identical complete sequences regardless of missing data
settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement Phase 6 (Voice Signaling) and Phase 7 (Hardening)

Phase 6 - Voice Signaling:
- handle_voice_signal(): relay VoiceSignal to target user via
  find_session(); anti-spoofing check on from_user; CALL_INVITE to
  offline recipient returns MT_ERROR to caller; other signal types
  are silently dropped when recipient is offline

Phase 7 - Hardening:
- net/rate_limiter.hpp: fixed-window RateLimiter (header-only)
- Per-session message rate limiter initialised on auth success;
  MT_CHAT_ENVELOPE, MT_VOICE_SIGNAL, MT_KEY_UPLOAD, MT_KEY_REQUEST
  all gated through it (429 error on excess)
- Listener: max_connections enforced before TLS handshake; per-IP
  connection rate limiter (sliding window, 1 min); active_connections_
  atomic counter tracks pre-auth + auth sessions
- Input validation: is_valid_user_id() (1-64 printable non-space
  chars); auth key length checks against crypto_sign_BYTES constants
- OfflineStore::cleanup_expired() deletes TTL-expired rows
- Server: hourly periodic cleanup timer via boost::asio::steady_timer
- Config: msg_rate_per_sec (default 20), conn_rate_per_min (default 10)
  added to [limits] section; server.toml.example updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add: click map to set route endpoints

Three ways to set From/To without touching the dropdowns:

1. Click any waterway (canal/river) on the map
   → popup snaps to nearest town on that route
   → 'From here' (green) / 'To here' (red) buttons

2. Click any town marker → sidebar opens
   → 'Set as From' / 'Set as To' buttons at the top

3. Change the dropdowns in the planner panel
   → pins update automatically on the map

Visual feedback:
- Green teardrop pin (A) = From, red pin (B) = To
- Nudge bar floats at the bottom showing both endpoints
  and an 'Calculate Route' button — no need to open the planner
- Swap button (⇅) swaps both dropdowns AND the map pins

Pins persist when the planner panel is closed so endpoints
remain visible while exploring the map.

commit.message:
fix: install slash commands to ~/.claude/commands/ instead of ~/.claude/skills/

Claude Code only recognizes slash commands in ~/.claude/commands/ (user-level)
or .claude/commands/ (project-level). The old ~/.claude/skills/ path was
invisible to Claude Code, so /tmux-* commands never appeared.

Also updates web-install.sh to use the same correct path.

commit.message:
feat: Add Google Search Console and PostHog integration to Workflow C

This commit adds comprehensive multi-platform analytics tracking capabilities:

**New Features:**
- Google Search Console (GSC) integration with OAuth 2.0 authentication
  - Search analytics (impressions, clicks, CTR, position)
  - Core Web Vitals tracking
  - Environment variable and file-based credential support

- PostHog product analytics integration
  - User behavior events tracking
  - Insights, funnels, and feature flags support
  - Project API Key authentication

**Changes:**
- Workflow C now supports 3 platforms: X/Twitter, GSC, PostHog
- Separate JSONL storage per platform for efficient querying
- Added `--source` parameter to sync_metrics.py (x, gsc, posthog, all)
- Removed systemd service configuration in favor of Linux cron jobs
- Updated README.md with comprehensive deployment guide using cron

**New Files:**
- src/growth_agent/ingestors/gsc_search_console.py - GSC API client
- src/growth_agent/ingestors/posthog.py - PostHog API client
- scripts/create_gsc_creds.py - GSC credential helper
- scripts/test_posthog.py - PostHog API validation tool

**Bug Fixes:**
- Fixed GSC token expiry parsing (datetime vs timestamp)
- Fixed PostHog insight field mapping (insight_label vs insight_name)
- Added missing Literal and timedelta imports in workflow_c.py

**Documentation:**
- Updated README with GSC and PostHog setup instructions
- Added cron job configuration examples for all workflows
- Updated FAQ with common issues (PostHog 401, GSC credentials)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add team-based permissions and team assignment for templates

submissions, folders) by team. Admins can create/manage teams, assign
users to teams, and assign templates to teams via preferences. Editors
and viewers only see resources belonging to their teams (or unassigned).
